name: Privilege Escalation: SUID/SGID Bit Set on System Binary
description: Detects when the SUID or SGID bit is set on a system binary in /bin, /sbin, /usr/bin, or /usr/sbin. Attackers set SUID to maintain elevated execution. MITRE T1548.001 - Setuid and Setgid.
query:
file where event.action in ("changed-file-permissions", "chmod") and
  file.path : ("/bin/*", "/sbin/*", "/usr/bin/*", "/usr/sbin/*") and
  process.name != "dpkg" and process.name != "rpm" and process.name != "apt"

name: Privilege Escalation: Sudo Shell Escape Attempt
description: Detects sudo invocations that spawn an interactive shell (bash, sh, python, perl, etc.) — a common GTFOBins technique to escape to a root shell. MITRE T1548.003 - Sudo and Sudo Caching Abuse.
query:
process where event.type == "start" and
  process.parent.name == "sudo" and
  process.name in ("bash", "sh", "zsh", "fish", "python", "python3", "perl", "ruby", "vi", "vim", "less", "more", "awk", "nmap")

name: Privilege Escalation: User Added to Privileged Group
description: Detects a user being added to sudoers, wheel, or admin group. This is a common persistence and privilege escalation technique used after initial compromise. MITRE T1098 - Account Manipulation.
query:
process where event.type == "start" and
  process.name in ("usermod", "gpasswd", "adduser", "net") and
  process.args : ("*sudo*", "*wheel*", "*admin*", "-aG sudo", "-aG wheel")

name: Privilege Escalation: Critical Auth File Modified (/etc/passwd, /etc/shadow)
description: Detects modification of /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, or /etc/group — the core Linux account database. Attackers modify these to add backdoor accounts or change root password. MITRE T1136.001, T1531.
query:
file where event.action in ("changed-file-content", "overwrite", "rename") and
  file.path in ("/etc/passwd", "/etc/shadow", "/etc/group", "/etc/gshadow") and
  not process.name in ("passwd", "shadow", "chpasswd", "useradd", "usermod", "userdel")

name: Privilege Escalation: Kernel Module Loaded (Rootkit Indicator)
description: Detects insmod, modprobe, or rmmod execution by a non-root process. Kernel module loading is a primary rootkit installation technique. MITRE T1547.006 - Kernel Modules and Extensions.
query:
process where event.type == "start" and
  process.name in ("insmod", "modprobe", "rmmod") and
  not process.parent.name in ("systemd", "update-initramfs", "dracut")
